content.problemStatement:
You have an N×NN \times NN×N character matrix AAA composed of lowercase letters, but you have forgotten the matrix. Fortunately, you still remember N2N^2N2 strings SijS_{ij}Sij​ (1≤i,j≤N1 \leq i,j \leq N1≤i,j≤N) of length NNN, and an N×NN \times NN×N integer matrix BBB .

For all 1≤i,j≤N1 \le i,j \le N1≤i,j≤N:


If Bij=0B_{ij}=0Bij​=0, SijS_{ij}Sij​ represents the string with the **smaller** lexicographical order between the string represented by the iii-th row and the string represented by the jjj-th column of array AAA;
Otherwise Bij=1B_{ij} = 1Bij​=1 and SijS_{ij}Sij​ represents the string with the **larger** lexicographical order between the string represented by the iii-th row and the string represented by the jjj-th column of array AAA.
Your task is to find the character matrix AAA. The input data guarantees that there is at least one possible matrix AAA. If there are multiple possible matrices AAA, output any one of them.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the matrices.
The next NNN lines contain NNN space-separated strings. The jjj-th string of the iii-th line is SijS_{ij}Sij​.
The next NNN lines contain NNN space-separated integers. The jjj-th integer of the iii-th line is BijB_{ij}Bij​




Output Format
For each test case, output the matrix AAA. If there are several valid matrices, output any of them.
constraints: SijS_{ij}Sij​ contains lower case characters only
There exists at least one valid matrix AAA
The sum of N3N^3N3 does not exceed 10610^6106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Let's show that the given output matrix is indeed a solution.
The matrix is: \\
abab\\ab
acacac

i=1,j=1i = 1, j = 1i=1,j=1 : String represented by 111st row is ababab while string represented by 111st column is aaaaaa. B11=1B_{11} = 1B11​=1, and so S11=S_{11} = S11​= lexicographically larger string which is ababab.
i=1,j=2i = 1, j = 2i=1,j=2 : String represented by 111st row is ababab while string represented by 222nd column is bcbcbc. B12=0B_{12} = 0B12​=0, and so S12=S_{12} = S12​= lexicographically smaller string which is ababab.
i=2,j=1i = 2, j = 1i=2,j=1 : String represented by 222nd row is acacac while string represented by 111st column is aaaaaa. B21=1B_{21} = 1B21​=1, and so S21=S_{21} = S21​= lexicographically larger string which is acacac.
i=2,j=1i = 2, j = 1i=2,j=1 : String represented by 222nd row is acacac while string represented by 222nd column is bcbcbc. B22=0B_{22} = 0B22​=0, and so S22=S_{22} = S22​= lexicographically smaller string which is acacac.

In fact, it can be proven that this matrix is also the only solution to this input.
